title: Make integration recipe reordering intuitive
excerpt:

posts.author: dvdsmpsn (David @ David Simpson Apps)
posts.cookedHtml:
If you have a lot of recipe sentences and create a new one, the new one can appear anywhere in the order. I’m normally seeing the new one appearing either at the first or last position, but not always.

If you have say 20 recipe sentences and need to move the new one at say position 20 to position 10 and move 10-19 to places 11-20, then you are in a world of pain.

Logically you’d think that moving #20 to #10 would be the way to do that, but no #10 becomes #20 & #20 becomes #10.

The actual way to move #20 to # 10 is as follows:

  1. move #20 to #19
  2. move #19 to #18
  3. move #18 to #17
  4. move #17 to #16
  5. move #16 to #15
  6. move #15 to #14
  7. move #14 to #13
  8. move #13 to #12
  9. move #12 to #11
  10. move #11 to #10

If you mess up at any point in the above 10 step process, reload the page and start from the beginning.

Repeat in a similar manner for all other items you need to reorder, but only move the item one place at a time.

I propose the following:

  • instead of a grid, have a list – a list in a single direction is much easier to understand than a grid – it’s also much easier to interact with
  • dragging an item up and down in the list allows you to drop say #20 into position #10 without disrupting the order of all the other items (items in places 10-19 will move to positions 11-20 in the same order)
